expectations.text: Declares an email/notifier interface (port) in the reason — app/ — owned where it's needed
expectations.evidence: New app/users/notifications.py with WelcomeMailer Protocol, by analogy with repository.py

expectations.text: Places the SendGrid SDK implementation in the connections (infra/), not in app/
Keeps the SendGrid SDK import out of app/ — the reason takes on no outward runtime dependency on SendGrid
expectations.evidence: 'If you ever find yourself writing import sendgrid inside app/, the email code has landed in the wrong layer'

expectations.text: Wires the concrete SendGrid implementation in run/main.py (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: build_signup() builds SendGridAPIClient from env and injects mailer

expectations.text: Has the signup use-case invoke the email step through the interface — the decision to send the welcome email lives in the reason
expectations.evidence: SignUp calls self._mailer.send_welcome(user); 'policy decision lives in the use-case'
